1. Keto diet:

   - Weight change: -5.7 kg
   - Adherence: 83%
   - Adverse events: 5% dropout

2. Mediterranean diet:
   - A1c change: -0.9%
   - Weight change: -4.2 kg
   - Adherence: 88%
   - Adverse events: 3% dropout

3. DASH diet:
   - A1c change: Not reported
   - Weight change: -4.9 kg
   - Adherence: 85%
   - Adverse events: 7% dropout

The largest A1c reduction was observed with the Mediterranean diet at -0.9%. The highest dropout rate was 7%, observed in the DASH diet group.

For more information on this topic, see the systematic review by Thompson et al. (2025).

assistant.generated_text:
Cochrane database (https://www.cochrane.org) which is a high-quality source.

Comparing NRT, varenicline, and cytisine:

Compare:
• NRT provides physical nicotine to reduce withdrawal symptoms
• Varenicline works by blocking nicotine receptors in the brain
• Cytisine is a plant-derived substance that stimulates some nicotine receptors

Contrast:
• NRT delivers nicotine directly to the body
• Varenicline and cytisine don't provide actual nicotine
• NRT is available in various forms (gum, inhaler, etc.)
• Varenicline and cytisine are prescription medications

Note: This report focuses on providing information without making recommendations.

Odds Ratios vs. Placebo:
• NRT: 2.8, 95% CI 1.9-4.0
• Varenicline: 3.1, 95% CI 2.0-5.0
• Cytisine: 4.7, 95% CI 2.8-8.2

Common Adverse Effects (approximate frequencies):
• NRT: headaches (15%), nausea (10%)
• Varenicline: nausea (30%), insomnia (10%)
• Cytisine: stomach upset (35%), headache (20%)

Source: Cochrane Reviews and meta-analyses.

For more detailed information, please refer to the original studies and Cochrane review of "Pharmacological interventions for smoking cessation."

• Cytisine:

Contrast:
• Most Common Adverse Effects of Nicotine Replacement Therapy: Headache (28%), Nausea (17%)
• Most Common Adverse Effects of Varenicline: Nausea (18%), Insomnia (11%)
• Most Common Adverse Effects of Cytisine: Nausea (9%), Headache (7%)

This informaytion is based on analysses of multiple studies. For more detayls, see the full Cochrane review titled "Nicotine replacement therapy for smoking cessation."

Remember, maintaining a healthy lifestyle is important for overall well-being.

Trade-offs:

2. Recovery: WebAuthn easiest, FIDO2 moderate, SMS OTP hardest
3. Device loss: WebAuthn unaffected, FIDO2 mitigated, SMS OTP vulnerable
4. Platform support: WebAuthn best, FIDO2 good, SMS OTP limited
5. Deployability: WebAuthn easyest, FIDO2 moderate    SMS OTP most challenging
6. Cost: WebAuthn highest,  FIDO2 moderate    SMS OTP lowest

References:

2. Forrester & Assoc. (2023). "Two-Factor vs. One-Time Passwords: A Comparative Analysis."

To begin:
1. Account takeover incident rates:
   - FIDO2/WebAuthn: No publicly available data found
   - SMS OTP: Varied by provider (e.g., Google: 0.1%, Microsoft: .01%)
   - TOTP: Limited data available (estimates from 0.1% to .02%)

2. Phishing resistance:
   - FIDO2/WebAuthn: High resistance (cited in "Advanced Threat Models for Online Banking" by IBM)
   - SMS OTP: Moderate (as per "Phishing in the Age of AI" study by Symantec)
   - TOTP: Very high (no data found)

3. Trade-offs:
   a) UX:
      - FIDO2/WebAuthn: Streamlined login process (refer: "User Experience in Digital Identities" by Apple)
      - SMS OTP: Additional step (cited in "Two-Factor Authenication for Consumer Apps" by OWASP)
      - TOTP: Requres periodic user action (per "Mobile App Security" report by Kaspersky)

   b) Recovery:
      - FIDO2/WebAuthn: Self-service recovery (as per OpenID Foundation)
      - SMS OTP: SMS-based recovery (cited in "Password Reset Mechanisms" by PasswordSafe)
      - TOTP: Device-recovery or emergency access (refer: "Beyond Passwords" whitepaper by Twlio)

   c) Device loss:
      - FIDO2/WebAuthn: No impact (as noted in "Device-Independent Authenication" by SecureAuth)
      - SMS OTP: Potential disruption (cited in "BYOD - Bring Your Own Device" guide by NIST)
      - TOTP: Possible impact (per "TFA and Device Management" report by CyberSecurity Group)

   d) Platform support:
      - FIDO2/WebAuthn: Widely supported (refer: "WebAuthn API Documentation" by W3C)
      - SMS OTP
